summary:
The persistence of thumb-sucking extends far past infancy through much of early childhood; therefore, "throughout childhood" accurately captures its duration based on current understanding. This behavior commonly continues from toddlers up to around age six but can persist further under certain circumstances. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related directly addressing when exactly thumb-sucking ceases being typical among children,

Thumb sucking usually lasts throughout early childhood, peaking before kindergarten then gradually diminishing though some may continue occasionally till older grades reflecting a prolonged normative phase spanning several crucial developmental stages prior stabilizing fully by mid-childhood generally speaking without significant functional impairment noted medically unless persistent excessive leading potential dental issues warrant intervention guidance if needed timely monitoring advised especially concerning long-term effects such malocclusions etc..
